# GenAI Normalizer Processor
| Status        |           |
| ------------- |-----------|
| Stability     | [development]: traces   |
| Distributions | [] |
| Issues        | [![Open issues](https://img.shields.io/github/issues-search/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-collector-contrib?query=is%3Aissue%20is%3Aopen%20label%3Aprocessor%2Fgenainormalizer%20&label=open&color=orange&logo=opentelemetry)](https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-collector-contrib/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3Aprocessor%2Fgenainormalizer) |
| [Code Owners](https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-collector-contrib/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#becoming-a-code-owner)    | [@kylehounslow](https://www.github.com/kylehounslow) |

<!-- end autogenerated section -->

Normalizes GenAI telemetry attributes from [OpenInference](https://github.com/Arize-ai/openinference) and [OpenLLMetry](https://github.com/traceloop/openllmetry) to the official [OTel GenAI Semantic Conventions](https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/gen-ai/) (v1.39.0).

## Why

The official OTel GenAI instrumentation covers [7 libraries](https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-python-contrib/tree/93bea2dde784/instrumentation-genai). [OpenInference](https://github.com/Arize-ai/openinference/tree/main/python/instrumentation) covers 30+ and [OpenLLMetry](https://github.com/traceloop/openllmetry/tree/main/packages) covers 30+ — each using incompatible attribute names for identical concepts. Without normalization, you can't build unified dashboards, compute per-agent metrics, or correlate traces across frameworks.

## What it does

Single-pass attribute canonicalization applied to both span and span event attributes:

| Category | Example |
|----------|---------|
| Schema collision resolution | `llm.usage.prompt_tokens` (OpenLLMetry) → `gen_ai.usage.input_tokens` |
| | `llm.token_count.prompt` (OpenInference) → `gen_ai.usage.input_tokens` |
| Span kind value mapping | `openinference.span.kind: "LLM"` → `gen_ai.operation.name: "chat"` |
| Type conversion | `llm.response.finish_reason: "stop"` → `gen_ai.response.finish_reasons: ["stop"]` |

34 attribute mappings across 2 profiles: `openinference`, `openllmetry`. All targets verified against the [OTel GenAI SemConv registry](https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/registry/attributes/gen-ai/).

## Configuration

```yaml
processors:
  genainormalizer:
    # Target OTel GenAI Semantic Convention version. Default: "1.39.0"
    semconv_version: "1.39.0"

    # Mapping profiles to enable. Default: [openinference, openllmetry]
    profiles: [openinference, openllmetry]

    # Delete source attributes after mapping. Default: true
    # When true, after mapping llm.model_name → gen_ai.request.model, the original
    # llm.model_name is removed from the span. Set false to keep both.
    remove_originals: true

    # Overwrite existing target attributes. Default: false
    # When false, if gen_ai.request.model already exists on a span before normalization,
    # it won't be overwritten by the value from llm.model_name.
    overwrite: false

    # User-defined source→target mappings. Applied after profiles, overrides on conflict.
    custom_mappings:
      my_vendor.model: gen_ai.request.model

service:
  pipelines:
    traces:
      receivers: [otlp]
      processors: [genainormalizer, batch]
      exporters: [otlp/backend]
```

### Configuration fields

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `semconv_version` | string | `"1.39.0"` | Target OTel GenAI Semantic Convention version |
| `profiles` | []string | `[openinference, openllmetry]` | Mapping profiles to enable |
| `remove_originals` | bool | `true` | After mapping `llm.model_name` → `gen_ai.request.model`, delete the original `llm.model_name`. Set `false` to keep both. |
| `overwrite` | bool | `false` | If the target attribute (e.g. `gen_ai.request.model`) already exists on the span, don't overwrite it. Set `true` to replace. |
| `custom_mappings` | map[string]string | `{}` | Additional source→target attribute mappings |

### When does `overwrite` matter?

Some instrumentation setups emit both legacy and native attributes on the same span. For example, during migration an OpenLLMetry-instrumented app might produce:

```
llm.request.model: "gpt-4o"          # legacy OpenLLMetry attribute
gen_ai.request.model: "gpt-4o-mini"  # already set by native OTel instrumentation
```

With `overwrite: false` (default), the existing `gen_ai.request.model: "gpt-4o-mini"` is left alone. With `overwrite: true`, it gets replaced with `"gpt-4o"` from the legacy attribute.

## Building

Build a custom collector using [OCB](https://opentelemetry.io/docs/collector/custom-collector/):

```bash
go install go.opentelemetry.io/collector/cmd/builder@latest
builder --config builder-config.yaml
./dist/otelcol-genai --config collector-config.yaml
```

## Testing

Unit tests:

```bash
go test ./...
```

Real agent test scenarios included in `test-agents/`, using OpenInference and OpenLLMetry instrumentation across multiple frameworks:

| Framework | OpenInference | OpenLLMetry |
|-----------|:---:|:---:|
| Strands | ✅ | ✅ |
| LangChain | ✅ | ✅ |
| LangGraph | ✅ | ✅ |
| CrewAI | ✅ | ✅ |

```bash
# Run all scenarios (requires AWS credentials for Bedrock)
./test-agents/run-all.sh
```

## Status

Development — working toward [donation to opentelemetry-collector-contrib](https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-collector-contrib/issues/46069).
